stem: Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to \infty} \frac{x^{[[n]]}}{[[b]]^{x}} \), comparing polynomial growth against exponential growth.
solution: Exponential growth \( [[b]]^{x} \) dominates any fixed power \( x^{[[n]]} \), so the ratio tends to \( 0 \): \( \lim_{x \to \infty} \frac{x^{[[n]]}}{[[b]]^{x}} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This assumes numerator and denominator grow at the same rate.
This has the wrong sign; the ratio of positive terms cannot be negative.
This mistakes the limit for the polynomial's degree.
This mistakes the limit for the exponential base.
This adds the two parameters.
This multiplies the two parameters.

stem: Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to \infty} \frac{(\ln x)^{[[p]]}}{x^{[[q]]}} \), comparing logarithmic growth against polynomial growth.
solution: Every power of a logarithm grows more slowly than every positive power of \( x \), so \( (\ln x)^{[[p]]} \) is dominated by \( x^{[[q]]} \) and the ratio tends to \( 0 \): \( \lim_{x \to \infty} \frac{(\ln x)^{[[p]]}}{x^{[[q]]}} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This assumes numerator and denominator grow at the same rate.
This has the wrong sign; the ratio of positive terms cannot be negative.
This mistakes the limit for the logarithm's exponent.
This mistakes the limit for the polynomial's degree.
This adds the two exponents.
This multiplies the two exponents.

stem: Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to \infty} \frac{[[b]]^{x}}{[[a]]^{x}} \), comparing the growth rates of two exponential functions with bases \( [[b]] < [[a]] \).
solution: Since \( \frac{[[b]]^{x}}{[[a]]^{x}} = \left(\frac{[[b]]}{[[a]]}\right)^{x} \) and \( 0 < \frac{[[b]]}{[[a]]} < 1 \), the larger base \( [[a]] \) dominates and the ratio decays to \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This assumes the two exponentials grow at the same rate.
This has the wrong sign; the ratio of positive terms cannot be negative.
This inverts the ratio of the bases.
This subtracts the bases instead of taking the ratio to a power.
This subtracts the bases in the wrong order.
This multiplies the bases instead of comparing their growth.

stem: Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to \infty} \frac{[[a]]^{x} + x^{[[n]]}}{[[a]]^{x}} \), which tests whether the additive polynomial term affects the exponential's dominant behavior.
solution: Split the fraction: \( \frac{[[a]]^{x} + x^{[[n]]}}{[[a]]^{x}} = 1 + \frac{x^{[[n]]}}{[[a]]^{x}} \). Since exponential growth dominates the polynomial term, \( \frac{x^{[[n]]}}{[[a]]^{x}} \to 0 \), so the whole expression \( \to [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This assumes the polynomial term vanishes entirely from the numerator, dropping the leading \( 1 \).
This mistakes the limit for the polynomial's degree.
This mistakes the limit for the exponential's base.
This overcounts, doubling the leading term.
This adds the degree to the correct limiting value incorrectly.
This adds the base to the degree instead of recognizing the polynomial term vanishes.
